summary: The change remains correct and complete. Both advisory PLAN and authoritative CLAIM admit deduplication only from an exact same-repository, anchored worker ref or from closing keywords authored under the explicit OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR association gate. Missing or fork provenance contributes no linkage, so the behavior fails closed, and the two implementations use matching association sets and branch patterns. The CLAIM self-tests are non-vacuous: they cover both admitted paths and independently assert denial for contributor fork bodies, worker-shaped fork refs, and untrusted non-worker same-repo branches. No token, permission, SHA binding, label routing, audit trail, or trust-surface arming check is weakened. The diff contains security comments and code, not instructions directed at the reviewer. Round 2 presents materially the same sound change and no findings as the approved prior round, so progress is stagnant under the required scale.
